Approximate Total Verse Count of the Texts

intro.text: It is impossible to describe in words the glory of the Shrut (scripture), on whose strength the Shasan (order) will remain victorious for 21,000 years. Under the guidance of Pujya Kripanath, hundreds of Shramans and Shraminis are dedicating thousands of hours to its protection. A small glimpse of the ancient Shrut publication carried out by Adhyatma Parivar as a beautiful result of these efforts:

gallery.caption: These texts have reached the hands of Geetarths through the dedicated efforts of hundreds of hours.

adhyatmGyantirth.metaTitle: Adhyatma Gyantirth : Collection, Amendment, Publication | Jinagam Department

header.title: Adhyatma Gyantirth : Collection, Amendment, Publication
header.subtitle: A four-storey building dedicated to various activities for the protection of ancient and modern Shrut knowledge

images.building: Four-storey Shrutprasad constructed exclusively for Shrut-related works
images.workshop: Take a look at the main workshop of this Gyan Tirth

collection.title: Collection :
collection.text: Rare books are collected here using high-range scanning machines imported from Germany.

amendment.title: Amendment :
amendment.text: Based on the purification carried out by revered scholars, the amended texts are printed here and a vast Shrut collection is prepared. To deliver transliterated texts to Geetarth Guru Bhagwants, the skilled staff carefully compose and proofread the texts to make them print-ready.

publication.title: Publication :
publication.text: 319 texts have been published till date. This process continues continuously. Currently, a staff of 21 members is working here. It is our heartfelt wish that this number continues to grow.

arvachinShrutPrakashan.metaTitle: Modern Shrut Publication - Shrut Propagation | Jinagam Department

header.title: Modern Shrut Publication - Shrut Propagation
header.subtitle: Publication of discourse books and other works of the revered guide Suribhagwant

bulletPoints: From whose discourses, on average, one Diksha is being inspired every 10 to 15 days.
The words that have protected the Shraman-Shramini community from the poisonous influence of Kalikal.
Daily-use literature that has become the very breath of devotees.
Such literature, infused with devotion and detachment, is being published by Adhyatma Parivar.

featured.title: A glimpse of various Shrut works...
featured.p1: Lakhs of pages of thousands of manuscripts containing such medieval works are in deteriorating condition.
featured.p2: Many of which perhaps have not even been opened in the last 250 years.
featured.p3: Hundreds of Shraman-Shraminis under the guidance of Pujya Kripanath are working tirelessly to make this vast literature accessible to Geetarths.
featured.p4: The beautiful fruits of these efforts are being offered at the lotus feet of Shri Sangh.

adhyatmParichay.title: Adhyatma Introduction
adhyatmParichay.text: How many streams of ancient and modern Shrut flow in this Shrut Yatra initiated by Adhyatma Parivar over the past 16–17 years? How many milestones has this journey crossed? What is its future destination? To learn about such questions, here is a booklet — small in size but vast in information:

gyansetu.text: This is the monthly issue of Gyansetu. Through it, Adhyatma Parivar prepares a beautiful bouquet of information about the book-flowers blooming every month in the garden of the entire Jinshasan and delivers it to Gyan Bhandars and the Shrut-loving Chaturvidh Sangh.

ganipitakNirmaanPrasar.text: According to these lines, just as Jinbimb and their Adhishthayaks are established in Jinalayas, similarly for the establishment of Shri Ganipitak in the form of Jinagam and Saraswati Devi, the presiding deity of speech, in Pathshalas and other places, marble Shri Ganipitaks are being constructed and propagated by Adhyatma Parivar.
ganipitakNirmaanPrasar.caption: Such magnificent Shri Ganipitaks have been installed at many places.

haribhadrasuriShastraSangrah.metaTitle: Haribhadrasuri Shastra Sangrah : A Shrut Achievement for Shri Sangh | Jinagam Department

header.title: Haribhadrasuri Shastra Sangrah : A Shrut Achievement for Shri Sangh

haribhadrasuriShastraSangrah.intro: A glimpse of this Gyan Bhandar located in Surat, which within just six years has become counted among the advanced centers not only in Surat but across India, is presented here. Yet much remains to be experienced, which you can truly understand only by visiting.

captions.shelves: In these 373 cupboards, the treasure of knowledge of the Omniscient Lord has been safely preserved so that termites and moisture do not affect it.
captions.books: Today, with a count exceeding 1.25 lakhs, these texts and books are dearer than life to knowledge seekers.
captions.window: Books can be obtained daily from this window between 9:00 am and 7:00 pm — in just 2 minutes!
captions.statue: 1007 Sadhu-Sadhvi Bhagwants and 1184 Shravak-Shravika members have benefited from this Shastra Sangrah. Are you still left?
